title: pilosella glaucescens subsp. branae
intro: Pilosella glaucescens subsp. branae, also known as the Alpine Mouse-ear, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for various conditions. Pilosella glaucescens subsp. branae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
